sources.label: Generac IR - Global supply agreement with leading hyperscale data center operator (Jun 2, 2026 - predates window)
TIKR - Jefferies upgrades Generac to Buy, $302 PT (May 22, 2026 - predates window)
CNBC - Jefferies turns bullish on Generac AI infrastructure upside (May 22, 2026)
StockTitan - Generac global data center backup power deal coverage

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at 71: no material news since June 6 - the hyperscale supply deal (Jun 2) and Jefferies/UBS upgrades (May 22 / May 5) all predate the window and were already reflected in yesterday's hold. Today's -3.2% is mild consolidation, not a setup change; small negative delta because the 'underfollowed' leg of the thesis keeps eroding as sell-side coverage and AI-datacenter narrative attention pile in after the +82% YTD rerating.
verdicts.bull: The global hyperscale supply agreement validates Generac as a credible second source in genset-constrained data center backup power, and C&I momentum (Q1 sales +12%, raised FY26 guide to mid-to-high-teens growth) gives fundamental backing to the move.
verdicts.neutral: Everything driving the stock - the deal, upgrades, guidance raise - is now public and well-covered; at +82% YTD the name is fairly tracked rather than hidden, leaving it a benchmark/monitor position rather than fresh alpha.
verdicts.bear: Post-rerating chase risk: a 'tight-supply, underfollowed' thesis no longer holds after CNBC/Jefferies/UBS attention; residential outage-driven core remains cyclical, and any hyperscaler order slippage or capex digestion would hit a stock priced for data center acceleration.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Disclosure of hyperscale agreement volumes/economics or a second hyperscaler win; Q2 earnings showing data center backlog conversion.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding unwind after the 82% YTD run if AI-power sentiment cools or the hyperscale deal proves smaller than the rerating implies.
